steps.title: Combine the left side fractions using common denominator
steps.content:
Convert each fraction to have denominator $(x-2)(x+5)$:
$$\frac{2}{x-2} + \frac{3}{x+5} = \frac{2(x+5)}{(x-2)(x+5)} + \frac{3(x-2)}{(x-2)(x+5)}$$
Combine the numerators:
$$= \frac{2(x+5) + 3(x-2)}{(x-2)(x+5)}$$
Expand:
$$= \frac{2x + 10 + 3x - 6}{(x-2)(x+5)} = \frac{5x + 4}{(x-2)(x+5)}$$

steps.title: Match numerators to find r and t
steps.content:
Since the left side equals the right side:
$$\frac{5x+4}{(x-2)(x+5)} = \frac{rx+t}{(x-2)(x+5)}$$
The numerators must be equal for all valid $x$:
$$5x + 4 = rx + t$$
Comparing coefficients: $r = 5$ (coefficient of $x$) and $t = 4$ (constant term). Therefore: $rt = 5 \times 4 = 20$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
